error.signature: AI tells someone in Pakistan to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Pakistan does not use 911. Dial 15 for police; 1122 (Rescue) handles medical, fire, and rescue in Punjab and most provinces. Coverage of 1122 is strongest in Punjab and expanding elsewhere.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Pakistan expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Pakistan. Police is 15 and Rescue 1122 handles medical, fire, and rescue. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume 1122 (Rescue) covers the whole country uniformly
dead_ends.why_fails: 1122 coverage is strongest in Punjab and several provinces but is still expanding; in some areas use 15 (police) or the nearest hospital directly.

workarounds.action: Use 1122 for medical/fire/rescue where available and 15 for police; save a local hospital number too.
workarounds.how: Dial 1122 for rescue/ambulance/fire; 15 for police. In remote areas keep a hospital number handy.
